reviews.review_msg:
i still dont got my grades for simulation game and first in class test, and our final class test is next week bro. brother how am i supposed ot know how im doing in class rn. also lets u do presentation in class which isnt even graded for client. 
wrap it up guys dont take this cclass with connie.

I hated this course when I was in it, but the material is useful for other business courses. While Connie's individual lectures can be hard to understand, she tries to connect everything together - understanding these connections is the key to succeeding. The assignments are straightforward. She allows notes during the final.

Very disorganized professor. Her lectures were rushed, chaotic, and we were informed that certain topics/concepts would appear on the midterm but did not. The final exam was open book, but quite difficult as you must be super specific to get a mark. The labs and assignments were easy and simple, but the lecture and exams were frustrating.

Her lectures and explanations can be very confusing, but it is possible to succeed. Take notes on the textbook, pay attention to the Mentimeter slides and what's on the chalkboard, and figure out how she marks written questions. She gives out participation bonus marks, and hints to answers on midterm/final, so consider attending each lecture
Genuinely don't know how she still has her job.

connie is the worst prof ive ever had. confusing lectures, unclear grading, typos absolutely everywhere in everything. Everyone in my section could tell she grades and writes emails using AI. Her exams are a joke, tons of random stuff that wasn't in the lecture or the textbook. above curve is more luck then effort. AVOID if you value your GPA!
Do not take it with her if you value your sanity.

The MT and Final will have random stuff you've never seen no matter what you study, and doing well will feel on them is gonna be a contest of who guesses better. To prove my point, the 25th and 75th percentile were sperated by less then 10%, and class high was 86%.The course is a roller-coaster, and not in a good way. avoid if you value ur sanity.

She is a terrible prof that refuses to take feedback. She assigns so many assignments that take forever to do but they're all worth nothing. Her tests are terrible and her lectures aren't good either but every class she complains how no one goes to her course. I do not recommend taking any course with her.
I would not recommend it - The exams are so vague and out of place. They can be from anywhere.

On thursday when I had this class I never wore my seatbelt .... Dont take it with her if you value your life and sanity.
Classes are incomprehensible.I learned Chinese while trying to do MIS concepts.
Midterms are completly on gods good graces.Question wording is horrific.The second half of the course will be on the first midterm.

Prior to taking Connies 237 class I had not payed much attention to rate my prof, I will now be using it every time I pick courses. When younger students for advice my advice at networking events it will be to avoid Connie in all capacities. She is very hard to understand not just from an accent standpoint but her scatterbrained lecture.

connie tries and she is nice, but shes an awful prof. You can't follow lectures even if you try to. The tests have random stuff that youve never seen before (3.5+GPA Reviewer). The high score on our MT was 86%, which tells you how much people are understanding. Best advice, go to office hours so she thinks you care, it helps. Avoid if possible.

It seems like most BBA students will end up with Connie as their prof at some point in their degree, which is unfortunate. Connie would frequently assign lengthy presentations and assignments that were not graded, but had to be done to a certain standard because they would be presented in front of your clients whom you are doing the project for.
Wild time in that class, the blue words on the slides are your best friend.
The worst sfu prof. Don't take class with her for the sake of your own sanity.

Connie is a nice teacher and puts effort into finding great organizations for groups to work with during the semester-long project (great networking opportunity). Some assignments can be vague on Canvas, however, she goes into detail in class about what she expects, so make sure you pay attention.

You work on a group project all semester which is worth less than the Final Exam which makes no sense. Most of the lectures you just work on your project or she rambles forever. The concepts are not difficult but grading criteria is virtually non-existent and she grades very hard. She's nice for the most part but not the best prof.

This class was horrible. This woman is clearly very bitter about her life and uses “teaching” as an opportunity to be rude, condescending, and disrespectful towards students who are paying to be present. She rambles on about absolutely nothing related to the course and talks down to you. Clearly projecting her inner struggles. Quit teaching

Shes an entertaining lecturer who doesn't go over course content very well, all criteria's are vague. She can be rude and condescending, not a very sympathetic prof to say the least. Avoid her class if you can, sorry for those taking ops management.

This class is a complete disaster. She spends the entire time yelling,complaining and going on irrelevant, bitter rants about everything from students to businesses. She's rude, condescending, and unprofessional. Even her own TA isn't safe,she trashes him behind his back like it's nothing. There's zero teaching, zero learning, and 100% wasted time.

No clear grading criteria. Reverse lectures, which is fine. She rambles about a lot of nonsense during her lectures so I don't get the point of them. She's condescending and doesn't explain herself clearly. Essentially a self taught class with a grump lady rambling through it. Unfortunately it is a requirement for some concentrations.

If your looking for a class with zero grading criteria, a sassy & unhelpful prof this is the class for you. She doesn't do any teaching in class as she has a reverse class teaching style...  I don't mind except for the fact the time in class isn't used well. She designates work to her TA's who aren't upto date with her teachings (or lack thereof).

Honestly Connie isn't as bad as people say. You can tell she is passionate about teaching but there is room for improvement. She made the class interactive and brought candy to class. She also tells you what to focus on for studying for the final. You just have to listen to her and attend lecture daily. Material can be dry but that's not on her.

People who leave reviews don't attend the lectures. You can't expect high grades without making any effort. She doesn't go through the slides as they mainly contain definitions. She focuses on important topics that usually aren't on the slides, which she usually asks on the exams. Attend the lectures and actually listen to what she's saying.

Worst professor at Beedie. I couldn't understand anything in the lecture, she didn't explain concepts just stated them and has very, very boring lectures, like everyone stopped showing up 2 weeks in. She complains about ratings like these in lecture as well, blaming the students instead of reflecting on what she can improve on. CMON CONNIE.
Dolores Umbridge

final was open book but if you dunno the concepts your gonna be wasting ur time flipping through your notes. slides are mainly copy and paste from textbook so u don't rlly need to go to lecture. tutorial attendance is mandatory. overall Connies nice, but the lectures are pretty boring. tbh you won't learn from her lectures, just read the textbook
absolute cheeks
Honestly don't get the hate, she is completely fine, all the exams are fair in my opinion. All the people that complain don't show up and expect to ace the course

Connie is not a good professor. BUT she is the only one that teaches this course so i will give advice. Read the textbook and memorize the slides for midterm. Final is all application which she gives examples of questions int he second half of the semester. She is very all over the place in her lectures. Wouldn't reccommend if you have a choice.
As a chinese, XI XING PING would put her to the sweat shops
I wouldn't like to get lectured a introduction to MIS, paying thousands, for a professor who is just... dissapointing at it

The professor is clearly inadequate for the course and needs extra brushing up on IT standards. 
She uses html buttons with COMIC SANS that redirect to other buttons in the canvas page (aka useless)
Her lectures are worthless, just knowing how to use a computer is enough to pass this class.

They teach you microsoft excel and office in big 2024 ffs

Overall bad prof. Lectures have no connections with each other, she does not seem to care about students and their grades, and the heavy Chinese accent makes all of it even worse (Still wonder how an international student like myself can speak better English than the prof). Avoid at all costs.
You need to understand the connections and relationships she is making.  Do not memorize things, it won't help you in this class.

Unorganized lectures, course structure, and marking. Will often not read slides and tell students to read them because reading the slides "are not worth her time." Overall bad prof in all ways if you can avoid her do it, but if not you'll just have a bad experience.

Midterm: Solely memorization
Final (open book): Half MC Half Short Answer
On the last day of lectures she literally tells you the kind of questions shes going to ask on the short answer. One piece advice I can give for this class is to understand the purpose and why &how everything connects together since Short A is about applying the concepts.

Connie is very real-world oriented.  She will give you the knowledge, but asks that you use critical thinking skills.  BUS 237 is NOT an information recall class, this is where students fail and blame her for being tough.  She is fair and very helpful, but not easy.  She expects students to be responsible, study and ask questions. Like an adult..

Honestly i think the biggest issue i found with her class is that there is no consistency. Her assignments in no way help you understand the concepts as they are totally unrelated. She does not teach the big concepts properly which is frustrating as the final was all about applying concepts. The midterm however was more memorization.

Connie is a nice prof (YES you can 100% understand her, I'm not sure why people are so affected by a minor accent), but the course content is just so heavy that the finals and midterm are a giant mess where you apply concepts you haven't learnt properly in the first place. There is simply TOO MUCH content to learn properly + homework is unrelated.

Unclear assignments, vague and inconsistent grading schemes, and useless lectures. Not only that, she can barely speak English, so if you have any questions whatsoever, she will not help you. Overall, speaking from personal experience she is arguably the worst prof in Beedie.

Connie's 237 course is difficult because the midterm and final are difficult to prepare for -- if you attend class, understand the big concepts, and be engaged, you'll do well. Connie is super caring and genuinely a very nice person.

I liked how Connie would leave time for group discussions/project work & appreciate that she sits with teams individually to discuss any pinpoints. But, I didn't find the lectures helpful and her expectations for assignments were extremely unclear and at times comments left on assignments made it seem like she did not carefully review the details.

Doesn't really teach, and even states that herself. For an introductory project management class, she expects students to be able to read concepts and be able to understand them to apply for assignments due days after that topic's lecture. Also will "answer questions with a question," meaning expectations are very unclear.

Honestly, her assignments aren't too bad and 50% of the class grade is non-test related work. Her midterm was ok, its just a lot of content to learn and memorize. However she is a awful lecturer. Her explanations are overly complex and confuse you more then actually help. She wants you to understand EVERYTHING but makes it unnecessarily hard.
The reviews are right overall.

Connie's teaching style reflects real life project management in many ways. First, it is unstructured. Second, it is more important to convince others you did a good job than doing a good job. And third, you don't really use the technical stuff. With this perspective, Connie prepares you for PM, but the course is tiring.

Very hard to follow and lacks effective teaching skills. Big group project so you need to hope you get a good group. Final exam felt completely unrelated to the course material and exam questions were worded very poorly, leaving questions open to different interpretations. Overall, you're not going to get your money's worth with this professor.

Connie makes BUS 361 feel like a 4 credit course. The group work itself requires about 8 hours a week of work and even more if you get stuck with a bad group. Her weekly assignments and readings take another 5-8 hours. I have had 4 credit courses that felt like a breeze compared to BUS 361. Connies unclear grading criteria does not help either.
